page: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is a General Purpose server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 64 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC-Turin processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz with 64 physical cores and a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3687.28, placing it in the top 10% of tested instances. Multi-core performance is also strong, scoring 118018.94 in stress-ng tests, which ranks in the top 25%. However, a key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation model, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-tenant workloads. Additionally, local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, requiring external storage solutions for data-intensive applications.

Given its high core count and balanced memory-to-CPU ratio, this instance offers resource density suitable for horizontal scaling, web serving, and general-purpose workloads that do not require dedicated CPU execution. The shared CPU model provides a cost-efficient alternative for multi-threaded applications, batch processing, and development environments that experience bursty rather than continuous high-utilization CPU demands.
description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is a General Purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 64 vCPUs on the AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3687.28 (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance at 118018.94 (top 25%). Tradeoffs include a shared CPU allocation model and a minimal 1 GB network storage allocation, which may limit sustained high-throughput workloads. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for resource-dense, non-continuous workloads, making it suitable for web serving, horizontal scaling, and batch processing.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is a General Purpose server with 64 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 256 GB RAM. It offers top-tier single-core performance but utilizes a shared CPU allocation and 1 GB network storage.

tagline: A high-core-count general purpose instance featuring balanced memory allocation and strong single-core performance for bursty, multi-threaded workloads.
